title: moorochloa eruciformis var. divaricata
intro: Moorochloa eruciformis var. divaricata, also known as the forked-petaled panic grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Moorochloa eruciformis var. divaricata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and treat various infections. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
